etymology.description: 二(二) (èr, "two") + 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") + 车(車) (chē, "vehicle; car") = secondhand car -> used car
etymology.interpretation: The term combines 二(二) (èr, "two" or "second") and 手(手) (shǒu, "hand") to indicate 'secondhand,' implying ownership change from one hand to another before reaching the current owner. 車(車) (chē, "vehicle; car") specifies the object, a vehicle, that has been previously owned.

examples.chinese: 他打算买一辆二手车(他打算買一輛二手車)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dǎsuàn mǎi yī liàng èr shǒu chē.
examples.english: He plans to buy a used car.

examples.chinese: 二手车市场非常活跃(二手車市場非常活躍)。
examples.pinyin: Èr shǒu chē shìchǎng fēicháng huóyuè.
examples.english: The used car market is very active.

examples.chinese: 购买二手车可以节省很多钱(購買二手車可以節省很多錢)。
examples.pinyin: Gòumǎi èr shǒu chē kěyǐ jiéshěng hěn duō qián.
examples.english: Buying a used car can save a lot of money.
